title: Add wireframe polyhedra geometry system
summary: The core system is ~75% implemented. Shaders, generator, system factory, quality integration, and main.ts wiring all exist. Remaining work: add aEdgeId attribute for per-edge color variation, expose positions getter for overlay compatibility, and write tests.
reviewerFeedbackAddressed: maxPolyhedra already exists in QualityProfile (low=3, medium=6, high=12) — no quality.ts interface change proposed
WIREPOLYHEDRA_ATTRIBUTES already exists with position + aRandom — step scoped to appending aEdgeId only
main.ts integration already complete (import, factory, modeManager, debug) — reduced to verification-only step
Edge count derived from polyhedron count × avg edges per shape, not from a separate quality field
No references to maxWireframeEdges anywhere in the plan

steps.title: Add aEdgeId attribute to shaderRegistry.ts
steps.description: Append { name: 'aEdgeId', itemSize: 1 } to the existing WIREPOLYHEDRA_ATTRIBUTES array. The array already has position (3) and aRandom (3); this adds a per-vertex edge identifier for color variation in the fragment shader.
steps.details: The attribute stores a normalized 0–1 value derived from (edgeIndex / totalEdges) so the fragment shader can apply slight hue offsets per edge, preventing uniform coloring across all wireframe lines.
acceptanceCriteria: WIREPOLYHEDRA_ATTRIBUTES contains exactly 3 entries: position, aRandom, aEdgeId

steps.title: Generate aEdgeId data in polyhedraEdges.ts
steps.description: Extend generatePolyhedronEdges() to also return a Float32Array of per-vertex edge IDs alongside the existing positions and randoms arrays. Each edge pair of vertices gets the same normalized ID = edgeIndex / totalEdges.
steps.details: Update PolyhedronEdgeData interface to include edgeIds: Float32Array. The generator already extracts edges from EdgesGeometry — the edgeId is simply the loop index normalized by edge count, written twice per edge (once per vertex of the line segment).
acceptanceCriteria: edgeIds length equals positions length / 3 (one float per vertex)

steps.title: Wire aEdgeId attribute into wireframePolyhedra.ts geometry creation
steps.description: When building BufferGeometry for each polyhedron's LineSegments, attach the edgeIds array from the generator as a BufferAttribute named 'aEdgeId'. Also expose a positions getter that collects world-space vertex positions from all active LineSegments meshes for constellation overlay compatibility.
steps.details: The system already creates position and aRandom attributes. Add: geometry.setAttribute('aEdgeId', new THREE.BufferAttribute(edgeData.edgeIds, 1)). For the positions getter, iterate meshes and collect transformed vertex positions into a flat Float32Array, matching the pattern used by pointCloud and ribbonField systems.
acceptanceCriteria: Each LineSegments mesh has aEdgeId BufferAttribute
System exposes a positions property returning Float32Array of world-space vertices
No WebGL errors on attribute binding

steps.title: Update wireframe shaders to use aEdgeId
steps.description: In the vertex shader, declare attribute float aEdgeId and pass it to the fragment shader via varying float vEdgeId. In the fragment shader, use vEdgeId to apply a small hue offset (e.g., ±0.05 * vEdgeId) to the palette hue, creating subtle per-edge color variation while preserving the overall palette identity.
steps.details: Vertex shader: add 'attribute float aEdgeId;' and 'varying float vEdgeId;', assign vEdgeId = aEdgeId in main(). Fragment shader: add 'varying float vEdgeId;', modify hue calculation to include '+ 0.05 * (vEdgeId - 0.5)' for centered offset. This is a minimal change — existing fog, bass/treble modulation, and opacity logic remain untouched.
acceptanceCriteria: Shaders compile cleanly with no WebGL errors
Per-edge hue variation is visible but subtle
Existing audio reactivity and fog behavior unchanged

steps.title: Write unit tests
steps.description: Add tests for the generator and system. Generator tests: verify all 4 shapes produce valid edge data with finite positions, correct array lengths, and edgeIds in 0–1 range. System tests: verify init creates LineSegments meshes up to maxPolyhedra count, draw updates uniforms, cleanup removes meshes, setOpacity works, and positions getter returns data.
steps.details: Follow existing test patterns (vitest, Three.js mocks). Generator tests should cover selectShape determinism (same seed → same shape) and all 4 shape types. System tests should verify mesh count equals maxPolyhedra from config, and that each mesh has position, aRandom, and aEdgeId attributes. Edge count per polyhedron varies by shape (icosahedron=30, octahedron=12, dodecahedron=30, tetrahedron=6) — total edges = sum across maxPolyhedra instances.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator tests cover all 4 shapes and seed determinism
System tests verify mesh creation, attribute binding, uniform updates, cleanup, and positions getter

steps.title: Verify existing main.ts integration and build
steps.description: Confirm that the existing main.ts integration (import at line 19, factory call at lines 129–134 using quality.maxPolyhedra, modeManager registration at line 141, debug modes at line 172) still works correctly with the aEdgeId additions. Run npm run build to verify clean compilation.
steps.details: No code changes expected in main.ts. The factory call already passes maxPolyhedra from the quality profile, and the modeManager entry is already registered. This step is pure verification that the upstream changes (steps 1–4) don't break the existing integration. Also verify no console WebGL errors by checking shader compilation in the build output.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build succeeds with no errors
Wireframe system initializes correctly within modeManager rotation

qualityNotes: maxPolyhedra (low=3, medium=6, high=12) already exists in QualityProfile and is already used by the wireframe system factory. Total edge vertex count scales naturally: each polyhedron generates shape-dependent edges (tetrahedron=6, octahedron=12, icosahedron=30, dodecahedron=30). With maxPolyhedra=12 and avg ~20 edges/shape, the high tier renders ~240 edges (480 vertices) — well within GPU budget for LineSegments.
riskNotes: Primary risk is shader compilation after adding aEdgeId. Mitigated by: (1) attribute is already registered in shaderRegistry before first render, (2) generator always produces the array, (3) fallback: if aEdgeId is missing, fragment shader hue offset is simply 0. Secondary risk: positions getter performance with many meshes — mitigated by caching and only recomputing when mesh list changes.
definitionOfDone: All tests pass including new generator and system tests
No console errors on normal load
4 base shapes supported: icosahedron, octahedron, dodecahedron, tetrahedron
Shape selection is seeded per session
Audio-reactive displacement works (bass macro, treble micro)
Depth fog applies consistently with point systems
Wireframe edges rendered as LineSegments (not points)
